mnemonics.title: Production System Components
mnemonics.content: P.R.W - Rules, Memory, Interpreter: Think 'P-roductiv R-eady W-orker' - The worker uses rules and memory to get products done!

mnemonics.title: Control Strategies
mnemonics.content: L.S.R - Lexicographic, Specificity, Recency: Think 'L-aws S-hould R-ule' - Laws should rule with order, specificity, and recent updates!

mnemonics.title: Recognition-Action Cycle Steps
mnemonics.content: M.R.A - Match, Resolve, Act: Think 'Mr. A' = Mr. Action - He MATCHES options, RESOLVES conflicts, then ACTS!

mnemonics.title: Chaining Types
mnemonics.content: FWD = Facts to Decisions, BWD = Backwards from Goal: Forward FOcus on Facts, Backward on Being Goal-oriented!

acronyms.usage: Stores current facts and assertions in a production system
Data-driven inference that works from known facts to conclusions
Goal-driven inference that works backwards from goals to facts
The main loop: Match-Resolve-Act in production systems
A problem-solving technique that reduces difference between current and goal state

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a waterfall or chain reaction - water (facts) flows forward, triggering more water (new facts) downstream. Start from source (known facts), follow path to destination (conclusion).
Imagine a detective working backwards from a crime (goal) - checking clues and evidence to reconstruct what happened. Start at end, trace back to beginning.
Imagine a traffic intersection with multiple cars (rules) wanting to go - a traffic cop (conflict resolver) decides which car goes first based on rules (lexicographic, recency, specificity).
Think of a whiteboard that gets updated - initial facts written, then new facts added/erased as rules fire. Always shows current state of problem-solving.

analogies.analogy: Think of a restaurant kitchen: Recipes (production rules), Kitchen counter (working memory), Chef (rule interpreter) who reads recipes, decides what to cook, and updates the counter with dishes.
Like a detective collecting clues at a crime scene - starting with evidence (facts), following each clue to discover new evidence until all facts are gathered (conclusion reached).
Like a doctor diagnosing a patient - starting with suspected disease (goal), asking questions and running tests (working backwards) to find supporting evidence.

Like a teacher choosing which student's question to answer when multiple students raise hands - uses strategies like who raised hand first (recency), who has more detailed question (specificity), or class monitor first (lexicographic).
